skill.name: m365-agents-ts
skill.description: Guidance for building TypeScript Microsoft 365 agents with AgentApplication, Express hosting, streaming responses, and Copilot Studio client integrations.
skill.summary: Build Microsoft 365 and Teams agents with the TypeScript Agents SDK.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and most are false positives caused by markdown code fences, TypeScript template literals, and reference links. The remaining concern is legitimate but sensitive credential and bearer-token handling in sample Microsoft 365 agent code, so publication is acceptable with a warning.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Token Handling in Agent Samples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes examples for Azure API keys, client secrets, and Copilot bearer tokens. This is expected for Microsoft 365 agent development, but users must avoid committing real secrets and should use managed identity or a secure secret store when possible.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed lines explicitly show secret-like placeholders and process.env access for cloud credentials. The context is legitimate sample configuration, so the concern is operational exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals as Ruby or shell backtick execution. No evidence found that SKILL.md executes these examples automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside markdown documentation or TypeScript strings. There is no executable Ruby, shell backtick command, or installer script in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to schema metadata, Microsoft Learn, and Microsoft GitHub sample pages. No evidence found that the skill sends data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are shown as sensitivity-label metadata or reference links. The file contains no fetch, HTTP client call, or exfiltration logic tied to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography finding points to the YAML description line. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithm use on that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 starts a metadata description block and does not name or invoke a cryptographic algorithm. This is a clear false positive in the available evidence.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed After Context Review
low_findings.description: The dangerous combination heuristic combined documentation-only npm commands, credential placeholders, and reference links. No evidence found of obfuscation, prompt injection, secret collection, or unauthorized network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The combined signals exist, but they are examples for Microsoft 365 agent setup. The reviewed file does not contain hidden instructions, encoded payloads, or automatic execution paths.

content.value_statement: Microsoft 365 agent projects require specific SDK setup, routing, hosting, and Copilot Studio integration choices. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for TypeScript agent implementation.
actual_capabilities: Explains package setup for Microsoft 365 Agents SDK TypeScript projects.
Shows AgentApplication routing patterns for conversation updates and messages.
Provides a streaming response example with Azure OpenAI.
Documents Copilot Studio client and WebChat integration patterns.
Links to Microsoft Learn and Microsoft sample repositories for verification.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include runnable project files.
API signatures may change, so Microsoft documentation should be checked before implementation.
Examples use placeholder credentials and require secure local configuration.
It does not cover deployment, tenant app registration, or production monitoring in depth.

use_cases.title: Create a Teams Agent Prototype
use_cases.description: Use the skill to draft a TypeScript agent with message routing and Express hosting.

use_cases.title: Add Streaming AI Responses
use_cases.description: Use the streaming example to design long-running responses with sensitivity labels and final cleanup.

use_cases.title: Connect to Copilot Studio
use_cases.description: Use the client integration notes to plan direct-to-engine and WebChat connection patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the m365-agents-ts skill to outline a minimal TypeScript Microsoft 365 agent with AgentApplication and Express hosting.

prompt_templates.title: Add Message Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the m365-agents-ts skill to design message and conversation update handlers for a Teams agent.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Streaming Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the m365-agents-ts skill to plan a streaming Azure OpenAI response flow with proper endStream cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Review Copilot Studio Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the m365-agents-ts skill to review a Copilot Studio client integration plan, including required environment variables and token handling.

output_examples.input: Create a plan for a TypeScript Teams agent.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering package installation, AgentApplication setup, Express hosting, message handlers, and verification against Microsoft documentation.
A design review that checks sensitivity labels, informative updates, streaming loop behavior, error handling, and final stream closure.
A checklist covering environment ID, schema name, client ID, bearer token provider, conversation creation, and WebChat connection flow.

best_practices: Verify current SDK signatures in Microsoft documentation before generating final code.
Keep credentials in environment variables or managed secret stores, never in source files.
Validate invoke payloads before logging, storing, or forwarding user feedback.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real Azure API keys, client secrets, or bearer tokens into prompts.
Do not assume sample package versions are current without checking npm or Microsoft docs.
Do not stream responses without a guaranteed final cleanup path.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build TypeScript agents for Microsoft 365, Teams, and Copilot Studio using the Microsoft Agents SDK.

faq.question: Does this skill include runnable source files?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples in SKILL.md.

faq.question: Which AI coding tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The examples handle cloud credentials and bearer tokens, which require careful secret management.

faq.question: Are the hardcoded URLs suspicious?
faq.answer: No evidence found. They are schema metadata and Microsoft documentation links.

faq.question: Should users verify SDK details?
faq.answer: Yes. Microsoft 365 Agents SDK APIs can change, so users should confirm current signatures before implementation.
